title: Reduce duplication in TestRunner suite execution

title: Fix quicksort bug in benchmarks
description: It appears to be a closure capture bug
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Paul Biggar
comments.text: Fixed closure capture bug. Root cause: simpleInferType didn't handle AST.Call expressions, so variables bound to function call results weren't added to TypeEnv and couldn't be captured by closures. Fix: Added FuncReturnTypes map to LiftState to track function return types, updated simpleInferType to look up return types for Call and TypeApp expressions. Quicksort now compiles (has runtime segfault - separate issue). All 3401 tests pass.

title: Stop configuring using env vars, and remove env vars from the compiler
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Paul Biggar
comments.text: Removed ENABLE_VERIFICATION_TESTS usage; verification now driven by --verification flag in TestRunner and run-verification-tests. Updated verification e2e docs and added ScriptHelper tests. Tests: ./run-tests --filter='Script Helper Tests', ./run-tests --no-build. Benchmarks: ./benchmarks/run_benchmarks.sh sum_to_n.

title: Cleanup tests to use stdout much less
description: A lot of the tests have their expected value as something like . However, the test suites nearly supports a simpler version: . The difference is the newline. I'd like to allow the test definition so that we can write  instead of the longer stdout-based test definition. Change the test parser to allow newline by default, then change all the tests to use the shorter, cleaner test definition
close_reason: Closed

title: Add a nicer bash prompt in the docker container, adding it to the dockerfile
Fix stale PassTestRunner comment and MIR pretty printing

title: Uncomment working tests
description: There are hundreds of commented tests in .e2e files, many of which probably work. Uncomment any working tests. When finished methodically work through non-working tests and make them work by improving and fixing the compiler

comments.author: Paul Biggar
comments.text: Uncommented 2 List.map tests in basic_let.e2e. Task needs more work - there are 1753 commented lines across e2e test files. Many are actual comments rather than disabled tests. Float list pattern matching and some other features are still broken, so some tests will remain commented.

comments.author: Paul Biggar
comments.text: Investigated commented tests. Most are either documentation comments or require unimplemented features (currying, partial application, runtime error handling, polyvariant types). The actual 'disabled tests' pattern is rare (~4 tests with DISABLED prefix). Main blockers are closure variable capture (affects quicksort-style tests) and Float list pattern matching (affects numerical tests). Task requires implementing missing features to uncomment more tests.

title: Optimization tests use shared pipeline and pinned IR printing
close_reason: Already done - OptimizationTestRunner uses shared IRPrinter with formatANF, formatMIR, and formatLIR

title: Stop copying tests to build directory
description: The compiler seems to be set up such that the tests run from the build directory, and we need to compile the code in order to copy the tests to the build directory. Instead, run the tests using the source directory, and stop copying them into the build diretory
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Paul Biggar
comments.text: Updated TestRunner to read test data from src/Tests, removed CopyToOutputDirectory entries from Tests.fsproj, and added a unit test guarding against copied test data. Ran ./run-tests (all tests). Benchmarks not run; project notes say they are currently broken.

title: Cleanup tests to use stdout much less
description: A lot of the tests have their expected value as something like `= stdout="5.50\n"`. However, the test suites nearly supports a simpler version: `= 5.50`. The difference is the newline. I'd like to allow the test definition so that we can write `= 5.50` instead of the longer stdout-based test definition. Change the test parser to allow newline by default, then change all the tests to use the shorter, cleaner test definition
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Paul Biggar

comments.author: Paul Biggar
comments.text: Added docs/fsharp-warning-flags.md with candidate F# warning/error flags and evaluation steps; left TODO() slots for warning IDs.

comments.author: Paul Biggar
comments.text: Quality gates: dotnet build and ./run-tests --no-build passed. benchmarks/run_benchmarks.sh reported validation/build failures for mandelbrot and quicksort; created issue c4d-wrt for mandelbrot.

title: Benchmarks: mandelbrot validation mismatch
description: The benchmark run reports mandelbrot validation mismatch: expected 15909, got 24091. Investigate compiler/runtime behavior for the benchmark and update expected output or fix the bug.
close_reason: Fixed. The 'validation mismatch' was not a compiler bug - the Dark mandelbrot code was counting escaped points (24091) while the Rust reference counts points in the set (15909). Swapped the return values to match Rust semantics. Now outputs 15909 and passes validation.

title: Factor shared CLI logic in Program.fs
close_reason: Already done - buildCompilerOptions and shouldShowNormal helpers exist and are used by compile/run commands

title: Install htop and bd via the dockerfile
close_reason: Added htop, jq, and beads (bd) to Dockerfile

title: In the benchmarks/CURRENT-STATUS.md, there are claims about various benchmarks failing in certain situations. Validate if that's true and update the documentation if not
close_reason: Validated and updated CURRENT-STATUS.md. Key findings: (1) spectral_norm and matmul now work reliably - segfault bug fixed, (2) nbody has no Dark implementation, (3) quicksort fails with closure capture bug not stack overflow, (4) mandelbrot still has float bug.

title: Normalize test scripts into a shared helper
close_reason: Already done - scripts/test-common.sh exists with build_tests, find_test_exe, and run_tests helpers
